Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a sample event log for two cases A1 and B2 The events for each case are sequential and similar in nature We need to group low level events into high level process steps

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Similarly for case B2 the same sequence of events occurs

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can observe that the process for each case is divided into several distinct phases

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Phase 1 Getting the raw material and preparing it for the next steps Events 1 5 Retrieving the raw metal sheet scanning its barcode placing it on the table aligning it and preheating it These are all preparation steps for the raw material

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Phase 2 Assembly welding Events 6 8 Picking up the welding tool and welding two corners This is the assembly step

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Phase 3 Quality Inspection Events 9 and 12 However note that the visual check is at the end Also the coating and drying steps are in between We might want to group the coating and drying as a separate phase

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Phase 4 Surface Treatment coating and drying Events 10 11 Applying protective coating and drying it

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Alternatively we might break down as 1 Material Preparation Events 1 5 2 Assembly Events 6 8 3 Quality Inspection Intermediate Event 9 4 Surface Treatment Events 10 11 5 Final Quality Inspection Event 12

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: But looking at the sequence the welding is followed by an immediate check weld integrity and then the coating is applied The visual check is after the coating is dried So the visual check is a final check

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Alternatively we might group the two quality checks together But they are separated by the coating process It might be better to have two separate quality steps one after assembly and one at the end

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: However in this case the coating and drying are after the welding and before the final visual check So Material Preparation Assembly Intermediate Quality Inspection Surface Treatment Final Quality Inspection

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Another approach sometimes in process mining we group by the main resource or by the logical flow

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the grouping by logical step seems appropriate

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We can also consider the time the events in each group are close in time So the groups are temporally clustered

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore it is better to have two separate quality steps for the two inspections

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Given the above I propose the following grouping High Level Step 1 Material Preparation High Level Step 2 Assembly High Level Step 3 Weld Inspection High Level Step 4 Surface Treatment High Level Step 5 Final Inspection

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The same grouping applies to both cases So we can just define the steps without repeating per case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The problem says the full log is large but the pattern is consistent So we assume the same steps occur in the same order for every case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The grouping is based on Temporal proximity Logical grouping Resource continuity But the primary factor is the logical grouping
